revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Daibu temple ruins]][[ja:大分廃寺]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Daibu temple ruins | native_name = 大分廃寺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Daibu temple ruins | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|35|05|N|130|38|09|E|region:JP-40_scale:20000|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Iizuka, Fukuoka|en|Iizuka, Fukuoka|qid=Q328605|lt=Iizuka, Fukuoka|1=Iizuka, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = temple ruins | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 8th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|35|05|N|130|38|09|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} {{nihongo|'''Daibu temple ruins'''|大分廃寺跡| Daibu Haiji ato|qq=}} is an [[:en:archeological site|archeological site]] with the ruins of the foundations of a [[Japanese pagoda]] from a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} located in the city of {{ill|Iizuka, Fukuoka|en|Iizuka, Fukuoka|qid=Q328605|lt=Iizuka, Fukuoka|1=Iizuka, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1941.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211360|title= 大分廃寺塔跡|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Daibu temple ruins are located approximately one kilometer east of the Daibu Hachiman-gu, a former shrine of [[Hakozaki Shrine]] which was built in 726 AD. The temple was constructed in the early 8th century, and was on a large scale; however, it does not appear in any historical records, and the name and history of the temple are completely unknown. It is located along the ancient road linking {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|qid=Q1330839|lt=Hakata Port|1=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka}} with the Tagawa region of {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|qid=Q1047134|lt=Buzen Province|1=Buzen Province}}. The ruins of the pagoda foundations have been known for a long time and are in almost perfect condition, making this a valuable temple site from the early Nara period. The pagoda foundation is a platform that is about one meter higher than the surrounding area, and there are 17 cornerstones centered on the core. From the spacing of holes, it is estimated that the pagoda was a three-story pagoda over 30 meters in height. The large core foundation that supported the central pillar of the pagoda has a posthole with a diameter of approximately 80-cm, and has two grooves facing outward, which may have prevented water from accumulating. Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s revealed that the temple area was estimated to be approximately 94 meters north-to-south and approximately 102 meters east-to-west. Traces of the {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|qid=Q17051413|lt=Main Hall|1=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} have been found to the left of the pagoda, indicating that the temple layout may have been patterned after [[Hokki-ji]] in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|en|Ikaruga, Nara|qid=Q379233|lt=Ikaruga, Nara|1=Ikaruga, Nara}}. In addition, {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}-style and [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]-style [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s with lotus patterns were excavated.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The site is approximately a 15-minutes walk from {{ill|Chikuzen-Daibu Station|en|Chikuzen-Daibu Station|qid=Q5097526|lt=Chikuzen-Daibu Station|1=Chikuzen-Daibu Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Sasaguri Line|en|Sasaguri Line|qid=Q5359259|lt=Sasaguri Line|1=Sasaguri Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.iizuka.lg.jp/rekishi/daibuhaiji.html Iizuka city home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/13017 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
<!-- {{URL|example.com|qq=}} -->
[[en:Daibu temple ruins]] {{wikidata link|Q109359748}} [[Category:Buddhist archaeological sites in Japan]] [[Category:Chikuzen Province]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Iizuka, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:奈良寺院]] [[Category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:福岡県の廃寺]] [[Category:飯塚市の歴史]]
